author: Maksim Volkov
description: Mark a note read or done with one click and see it in the file explorer: finished notes are grayed out with a checkmark. Any frontmatter property can style files via CSS snippets, updated instantly. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Maksim Volkov
manifest.description: Mark a note read or done with one click and see it in the file explorer: finished notes are grayed out with a checkmark. Any frontmatter property can style files via CSS snippets, updated instantly.
manifest.name: Mark as Read

name: Mark as Read

releases.author: hemy301
releases.description: The plugin is now called **Mark as Read** (formerly Explorer Property Attributes). The name and description lead with what most users come for: mark a note read or done with one click, see it grayed out with a checkmark in the file explorer. Nothing else changes — the plugin id stays `explorer-property-attributes`, existing installs keep updating, and all settings are preserved.

releases.author: hemy301
releases.description: Fixes 1.3.0's **Offer marking in every note** doing nothing on current Obsidian builds: `getAllPropertyInfos` now returns `{ name, widget, occurrences }` instead of the older `{ name, type, count }`, so the vault-usage detection skipped every property and the footer was only shown in notes that already had a checkbox property. Both API shapes are now supported.

releases.author: hemy301
releases.description:
**Mark any note with one click — no per-note setup.**

Previously the footer bar appeared only in notes that already had a checkbox property, so every note had to be prepared by hand before it could be marked. Now the done-marker properties (the **Gray out finished notes** list: `read`, `done`, `finished`, `complete`, `archived`, `прочитано` by default) that are already used somewhere in the vault are offered in **every** note's footer, and the first click creates the property in that note's frontmatter.

Mark one note once — from then on any note in the vault can be marked with one click.

Controlled by the new **Offer marking in every note** setting (on by default; settings saved by pre-1.2.0 versions keep their old behavior).

releases.author: hemy301
hemy301
releases.description: The note footer toggle now looks like a proper button (pill with border and background, green when checked) instead of a plain checkbox row that was easy to mistake for status-bar text.

releases.author: hemy301
releases.description:
New: **Note footer toggles** — list boolean properties in the new setting and every note that has one gets a checkbox bar pinned to the bottom of its pane. Click it to flip the property right in the frontmatter (via Obsidian's `processFrontMatter`) — no scrolling to the properties panel, no extra plugins.

The release now ships `styles.css` (Obsidian loads it automatically; needed for the footer bar).

releases.author: hemy301
releases.description:
Clearer, benefit-first description in the catalog and README; animated demo GIF.
No functional changes to the plugin code.

releases.author: hemy301
releases.description:
Initial release.

- Expose chosen frontmatter properties as `data-link-<property>` attributes on file-explorer items
- Instant updates when a property changes (metadata cache events + explorer MutationObserver)
- Supercharged Links-compatible attribute format — existing CSS snippets work unchanged
- Clean removal of attributes on setting change / plugin disable
